rulings.Case Title: JAMES BARAGAR et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Dismiss Or Stay For Forum Non Conveniens
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, November 1, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Honeywell International Inc.¿s motion to dismiss or stay for forum non conveniens is denied. At this late stage of the case, the balance of the relevant considerations ¿ including all the work that has been done by the parties in reliance on this case being tried in San Francisco and avoidance of additional costs and delay associated with the filing and prosecution of a new case in Wyoming ¿ weigh strongly in favor of retaining the case in this forum. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN COYLE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, November 1, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant FDCC California, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. The declaration of Mr. Cook creates a triable issue whether Mr. Coyle was exposed to asbestos-containing fireproofing attributable to defendant. The objections to Mr. Cook¿s declaration are overruled. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN COYLE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, November 1, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant New Albertson¿s, Inc., (SUED HEREIN AS ALBERTSON¿s)'s motion for summary judgment is denied. By not presenting responses to contention discovery with its moving papers, defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Coyle was exposed to asbestos-containing materials or products attributable to defendant. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: FLOYD CZARNICK et al VS. ALLIED PACKING et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, November 1, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Foster Wheeler LLC¿s motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication. The court is informed that Foster Wheeler is the only remaining defendant and the parties have stipulated to extend the five year statute for at least a week. Please confirm whether this information is accurate by sending an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4pm today. If so confirmed, the hearing on Foster Wheeler¿s motion will be continued to Tuesday November 2, the court will issue a tentative ruling on Monday November 1 and the parties need not attend the trail call at 11:15am on Monday November 1.. If not confirmed, the parties must appear for a hearing on the motion on Monday November 1 at 9:30 a.m. = (220/HEK)
